notes: Uplay launches fine for me. Game starts to launch but when it reaches "Warning: Read before playing" It crashes after a second.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: I've made it to the main menu. Only catch is you must set UPlay to be in offline mode. Maybe even steam. I suspect that the reason its not booting while online is due to the EasyAntiCheat software. There's a file called loader.log for EAC that contains a link to a page that doesnt exist. It believe the client is "wine64" instead of something like windows. But from my testing offline while attempting to play solo the game locks up in the loading screen.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: The game gets through Uplay's launcher just fine, shows a splash screen, and then silently crashing.  Steam reports that the game is still running until you close Uplay.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes:
Game tested through Lutris using tkg-protonified-4.6 wineprefix.

It has problems connecting to Ubisoft servers, so Multiplayer was not tested.
Singleplayer works with no issues (about an hour of gameplay).
No graphical glitches or performance issues on Very High settings.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: Also keeps saying ubisoft server unavailable. Uyuni-00090 sometimes it will run the menu till it fails to find a server, sometimes it just closes as soon as you hit OK.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes:
Does not work with default Proton eg. 4.2-9, runs through Steam if you are willing to install Proton-tkg, then manually select that as the proton version through Steam. Uplay online does not yet work, however the single player does work with an occasional crash or freeze. On Ultra settings, performance is good, 60+ in many areas, 30 in some (Windows seems to have a little of that as well) Hopefully a future proton version will allow this to work. Another thing that may be useful, open winecfg and under Drives, add a new drive with the Wildlands directory, this allows Wildlands to be launched from Uplay. Thanks to TK-Glitch for his work

https://github.com/Tk-Glitch/PKGBUILDS/tree/master/proton-tkg
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: Uplay launched successfully. On first run Uplay only ask to sign in with Uplay account, then Uplay window remains open albeit laggy. Game shows up in Uplay library but not running, despite Steam report it's running. Exit Uplay and game is no longer running in Steam. On second attempt Uplay launched the game and synchronized save files successfully, but game stuck at splash screen with no activity. KSysguard shows GRW.exe as zombie, and had to terminate process.

notes: It runs perfectly without any problem out of the box (it crashed once at the intro but worked afterwards) it only works in single play because it can't reach the ubisoft serves but as a single player game works very well.

notes: After latest Proton update works flawless
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: Game loads after a few minor issues and hangups but ultimately only offline mode works. Online mode gives error that it cannot connect to Ubisoft Servers. Certainly a HUGE improvement over previous proton versions where it wouldnt' even load the game at all.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: It works great offline (singleplayer), but doesn't work online because the game can't access Ubisoft servers.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands

notes: Basic game functionality is solid, single player works perfectly with some minor tweaks. The launcher launched both Ubisoft Player and a "toolbar" application holding the Ubisoft Player Icon, in-game this led to a weird graphical/mouse pointer issue that resulted in the mouse chopping across the screen(not an fps issue). After closing/minimizing the other windows(without closing the game), the mouse issue was fixed and single player worked 100%. Online, as others have stated, is thoroughly unplayable.
title: Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon® Wildlands
